overall.description: Static display of an ancient marble relief sculpture within a video game museum interface, depicting Asklepios, Hygeia, and a sacred snake from the sanctuary at Epidauros. The scene features educational text overlays describing the Tholos structure and snake symbolism, with subtle camera movement examining the artifact details.

scene_state.functional_zone: museum exhibit displaying ancient marble relief

events.description: additional descriptive text about healing rituals appears at the bottom of the display

agents.ego_description: stationary observation of the marble relief sculpture, examining the carved details of the figures and snake

entered.detail: semicircular marble relief depicting two robed figures and a large coiled snake, carved in classical Greek style
neutral-toned stone wall serving as the exhibit backdrop
stone base supporting the marble relief sculpture

segments.scene_description: The viewer observes a static museum display featuring an ancient marble relief from the sanctuary of Asklepios at Epidauros. The sculpture shows two standing figures and a sacred snake coiled around a raised structure. Warm artificial lighting highlights the carved details of the stone. At approximately sixteen seconds, additional text appears describing healing rituals associated with the site.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot presents a steady examination of an ancient marble relief that transitions from a purely visual study into an educational display. It begins with a focused view of the sculpture from the sanctuary of Asklepios at Epidauros, where warm artificial lighting emphasizes the carved figures and a sacred snake coiled around a raised structure. As the camera maintains its position, the visual experience shifts with the gradual emergence of descriptive text overlays. The scene finally expands its context as these annotations appear, detailing the healing rituals associated with the site while the lighting continues to highlight the intricate stone details.
